sections.content: The images show no visible change between step 156 and step 157 - both show the same merged purple blocks. Let me check if SPACE actually did anything by looking at the segmentation.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check if SPACE did anything
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
print("Current nodes:")
for n in nodes:
    if n['pixels'] > 10:
        print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} boundary={n['boundary'][:4]}")

# Compare with before SPACE
if last_transition:
    prev_seg = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation
    print(f"\nBefore: {len(prev_seg['nodes'])} nodes, After: {len(nodes)} nodes")
    # Count pixels by color
    prev_colors = {}
    for n in prev_seg['nodes']:
        prev_colors[n['color']] = prev_colors.get(n['color'], 0) + n['pixels']
    curr_colors = {}
    for n in nodes:
        curr_colors[n['color']] = curr_colors.get(n['color'], 0) + n['pixels']
    print(f"Before colors: {prev_colors}")
    print(f"After colors: {curr_colors}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

- id=4: c (charcoal) 16px at [2,41]-[6,45] - right UI button
But the actual colored parts... Let me check the history for when I clicked buttons. Previously I clicked the right button to make it purple. I need to click the left button to make it white.
Actually wait, let me look at the images more carefully. The UI shows two small squares at the top right. The left one appears purple and the right one white. But I need to find their exact positions.
Let me look at the ascii to find the UI button positions.
